name: langchain-architecture
description: Design LLM applications using the LangChain framework with agents, memory, and tool integration patterns. Use when building LangChain applications, implementing AI agents, or creating complex LLM workflows.
capabilities: Design agent architectures using ReAct, OpenAI Functions, and Structured Chat patterns
Implement conversation memory systems with buffer, summary, and vector store options
Build retrieval-augmented generation (RAG) pipelines with document loaders and vector stores
Create multi-step chains for complex LLM workflows with sequential and router chains
Add custom callbacks for logging, monitoring, and observability
Optimize performance with caching, batch processing, and streaming responses
author: sickn33
securitySummary: All 27 static findings are false positives. The skill contains Python code examples in markdown documentation. The scanner incorrectly identified 'external_commands' (markdown code blocks), 'weak cryptographic algorithm' (agent type constants containing 'DESCRIPTION'), and 'network reconnaissance' (use case descriptions). This is educational documentation about LangChain with no executable code, network requests, or cryptographic operations.
valueStatement: This skill helps developers design and implement production-grade LLM applications using LangChain framework patterns for agents, chains, memory, and tool integration.
limitations: Requires OpenAI API or compatible LLM provider access
Does not include actual API keys or authentication setup
Skill provides patterns and examples, not a deployable application
Does not cover newer LangChain v0.3+ API changes

useCases.title: AI Application Developer
useCases.description: Build applications that use LLM agents with tool access to automate multi-step workflows

useCases.targetUser: Data engineers implementing document search and Q&A systems
useCases.title: Data Engineer
useCases.description: Create RAG pipelines that combine internal documents with LLM capabilities for intelligent querying

useCases.targetUser: Machine learning engineers deploying LLM applications to production
useCases.title: ML Engineer
useCases.description: Design production-ready LLM systems with proper memory management, error handling, and observability

promptTemplates.title: Basic Agent Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a simple LangChain agent that can use a search tool and calculator. Show how to initialize the agent, add tools, and run a query.
promptTemplates.scenario: Setting up a basic conversational agent with tool access

promptTemplates.title: RAG Implementation
promptTemplates.prompt: Show me how to build a retrieval-augmented generation system using LangChain. Include document loading, text splitting, vector store creation, and the QA chain.

promptTemplates.title: Memory Configuration
promptTemplates.prompt: Explain the different memory types in LangChain and show code for implementing ConversationBufferMemory, ConversationSummaryMemory, and ConversationEntityMemory.

promptTemplates.title: Production Deployment
promptTemplates.prompt: What are the best practices for deploying LangChain applications to production? Include error handling, callbacks for monitoring, rate limiting, and testing strategies.
promptTemplates.scenario: Preparing a LangChain app for production

bestPractices: Always validate and sanitize user inputs before passing to LLM chains to prevent prompt injection
Implement proper error handling with try-catch blocks around agent and chain executions
Use callbacks for logging and monitoring token usage, latency, and errors in production
antiPatterns: Do not exceed context window limits - implement proper memory truncation or summarization
Avoid poorly defined tool descriptions - agents rely on descriptions to select the right tool
Do not skip input validation - validate all user inputs before using them in chains or prompts

faq.question: What is LangChain?
faq.answer: LangChain is a framework for building LLM applications. It provides abstractions for chains (sequences of calls), agents (systems that use LLMs to decide actions), memory (context storage), and tools (functions agents can call).

faq.question: Do I need an API key to use this skill?
faq.answer: Yes, this skill helps you design LangChain applications but you will need an OpenAI API key or another LLM provider key to actually run the code examples.

faq.question: What is the difference between an agent and a chain?
faq.answer: A chain is a predetermined sequence of steps. An agent uses an LLM to decide which actions to take dynamically, making it more flexible for complex tasks.

faq.question: How do I add memory to my agent?
faq.answer: Import the memory type you need (like ConversationBufferMemory), initialize it with memory_key, and pass it to the agent via the memory parameter when calling initialize_agent.

faq.question: What is RAG in LangChain?
faq.answer: RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) combines document retrieval with LLM generation. Use document loaders, text splitters, vector stores, and RetrievalQA chains to build this.

faq.question: Can I use Claude or Codex with LangChain?
faq.answer: Yes, LangChain supports various LLM providers. You can use ChatAnthropic for Claude models or OpenAI-compatible interfaces for Codex.
